characters.name: The Time Traveller
characters.description: Unnamed inventor who hosts dinner parties and demonstrates a time machine to his guests. Builds a full-scale machine in his laboratory.

characters.name: The Narrator
characters.description: First person frame narrator, one of the dinner guests, observant and somewhat skeptical of the Time Traveller's claims.

characters.name: Filby
characters.description: Argumentative dinner guest with red hair who disputes the Time Traveller's theories.

characters.name: The Psychologist
characters.description: Dinner guest who presses the lever on the model time machine during the first demonstration.

characters.name: The Medical Man
characters.description: Pragmatic dinner guest, skeptical of the time travel demonstration.

characters.name: The Provincial Mayor
characters.description: A dinner guest at the Time Traveller's gatherings.

voice_profile.tone: scientific and philosophical, measured Victorian prose

settings.name: The Time Traveller's house
settings.description: Drawing room, smoking room, and laboratory where the dinner parties and demonstrations take place.

settings.name: The laboratory/workshop
settings.description: The Time Traveller's workshop where the full-scale time machine is being built.

plot_threads.thread: The Time Traveller's mysterious invention and demonstration of the model time machine
Skepticism of the dinner guests about the fourth dimension and time travel
The Time Traveller's disappearance and return in a disheveled, injured state

world_rules: Time is a fourth dimension that can be traversed just like the three spatial dimensions
The time machine has two levers — one for forward travel, one for backward
Consciousness moves along the time dimension; the machine enables physical travel
